description: Numeric-payload canonicalization for v0.3 payload_sha256 (§9.2). A response.value / state carrying numbers MUST canonicalize to byte-identical RFC 8785 JCS across implementations, so the digest agrees. Pins the canonical JCS + digest for a representative numeric set: integer (42), negative fraction (-0.001), decimal (1234.5), small-exponential (1e-7), max-safe integer (2^53-1), and a nested array/object. RFC 8785 §3.2.2.3 number formatting == ECMAScript Number::toString over the IEEE-754 double value; a non-JS signer MUST use a conformant JCS library to match (and MUST preserve string content as-is — RFC 8785 §3.1 does NOT normalize Unicode).
obligation: canonicalize({response, state}) MUST equal `payload_canonical_jcs` byte-for-byte; the lowercase-hex SHA-256 of those bytes MUST equal `payload_sha256`. Numbers are serialized as IEEE-754 doubles per RFC 8785 §3.2.2.3 (ordinary decimals like -0.001 / 1e-7 canonicalize deterministically — they need not be exact doubles). Precision caveat: an application needing an integer beyond ±(2^53-1) exactly must carry it as a string, since a JSON number is a double.
